questions.subject: General Aptitude
questions.stem: Fill in the blank: 'Although the project was delayed, the team remained ____ about meeting the final deadline.'
questions.solution:
**Concept.** 'Although' sets up a contrast: despite a setback, a positive attitude persisted.

**Working.** The contrast with the delay calls for a positive word — 'optimistic'.

**Answer.** optimistic.

questions.subject: General Aptitude
questions.stem: The probability of getting a head in a single toss of a fair coin is _____ .
questions.solution:
**Concept.** Probability = favourable / total equally likely outcomes.

**Working.** $\dfrac{1}{2}=0.5$.

**Answer.** $0.5$.

questions.subject: General Aptitude
questions.stem: In the right triangle shown, the legs are $6$ and $8$. Compute the hypotenuse.

figure.caption: Right triangle

questions.subject: General Aptitude
questions.stem: What is $35\%$ of $240$?

questions.subject: General Aptitude
questions.stem: The idiom in 'The new exploration plan was a shot in the dark' means:
options: a wild guess with little chance of success
questions.solution:
**Concept.** Idioms carry a figurative meaning distinct from their literal words.

**Working.** 'A shot in the dark' is an attempt made without enough information — essentially a wild guess.

**Answer.** a wild guess with little chance of success.

questions.subject: General Aptitude
questions.stem: In a group of $50$ people, $30$ like tea, $25$ like coffee and $10$ like both. The number who like neither is _____ .
questions.solution:
**Concept.** Inclusion-exclusion, then subtract from the total.

**Working.** Tea or coffee $=30+25-10=45$; neither $=50-45=5$.

**Answer.** $5$.

questions.subject: General Aptitude
questions.stem: In a row of $20$ students, A is $7$th from the left. A's position from the right is
questions.solution:
**Concept.** Position from right $=$ total $-$ position from left $+1$.

**Working.** $20-7+1=14$.

**Answer.** 14th.

questions.subject: General Aptitude
questions.stem: The square-dot pattern grows as $1, 4, 9, \ldots$ (perfect squares). The number of dots in the next figure is _____ .

figure.caption: Square dot arrangements of 1, 4 and 9 dots.
figure.markup: <svg viewBox='0 0 240 90' xmlns='http://www.w3.org/2000/svg' role='img'><circle cx='25' cy='45' r='4' fill='#6366f1'/><g fill='#6366f1'><circle cx='80' cy='35' r='4'/><circle cx='95' cy='35' r='4'/><circle cx='80' cy='50' r='4'/><circle cx='95' cy='50' r='4'/></g><g fill='#6366f1'><circle cx='150' cy='25' r='4'/><circle cx='165' cy='25' r='4'/><circle cx='180' cy='25' r='4'/><circle cx='150' cy='40' r='4'/><circle cx='165' cy='40' r='4'/><circle cx='180' cy='40' r='4'/><circle cx='150' cy='55' r='4'/><circle cx='165' cy='55' r='4'/><circle cx='180' cy='55' r='4'/></g><text x='215' y='48' font-size='24' fill='#94a3b8'>?</text></svg>

questions.solution:
**Concept.** The terms are consecutive perfect squares $n^2$.

**Working.** $1=1^2$, $4=2^2$, $9=3^2$, so the next is $4^2=16$.

**Answer.** $16$.

questions.subject: General Aptitude

questions.subject: Igneous Petrology
questions.stem: A plagioclase has An$_{40}$. Its series index (0=albite…5=anorthite, by 20% steps An0/10/30/50/70/90) is _____.
questions.solution:
**Concept.** Albite(0–10), oligoclase(10–30), andesine(30–50), labradorite(50–70), bytownite(70–90), anorthite(90–100).

**Working.**
$$An_{40}\Rightarrow 2.$$

**Answer.** $2$

questions.subject: Sedimentology & Sedimentary Petrology
questions.stem: A sand has $d_{60}=0.6\,$mm and $d_{10}=0.1\,$mm. Its uniformity coefficient $C_u$ is _____.

questions.subject: Applied Geology · Remote Sensing & GIS
questions.stem: In a GIS, a continuously varying surface such as elevation is best stored as a
questions.solution:
**Concept.** Continuous fields (DEMs) are efficiently stored as rasters.

**Working.**
Raster.

**Answer.** Raster

questions.stem: For $\sigma_1=90$ and $\sigma_3=50\,$MPa, the mean stress (centre of Mohr circle) is _____ MPa.

questions.stem: For a rock with internal friction angle $\phi=20^\circ$, conjugate shear (fault) planes form at _____ degrees to $\sigma_1$.

questions.stem: Beds of $1600\,$Ma are overlain unconformably by beds of $541\,$Ma. The hiatus is _____ Myr.

questions.subject: Engineering & Environmental Geology
questions.stem: An aquifer has hydraulic conductivity $K=0.00001\,$m/s and saturated thickness $30\,$m. Its transmissivity is _____ ×10⁻³ m²/s.

questions.subject: Sedimentology & Sedimentary Petrology
questions.stem: A sand has $d_{60}=0.9\,$mm and $d_{10}=0.2\,$mm. Its uniformity coefficient $C_u$ is _____.

questions.subject: Metamorphic Petrology
questions.stem: The metamorphic facies characterised by high pressure and low temperature is
questions.solution:
**Concept.** Blueschist = high-P/low-T (subduction); granulite = high-T; hornfels = contact.

**Working.**
Blueschist.

**Answer.** Blueschist

questions.subject: Metamorphic Petrology
questions.stem: In Barrovian metamorphism, the index mineral marking the highest grade among these is
questions.solution:
**Concept.** Barrovian zones: chlorite → biotite → garnet → staurolite → kyanite → sillimanite.

**Working.**
Sillimanite.

**Answer.** Sillimanite

questions.subject: Igneous Petrology
questions.stem: A plagioclase has An$_{70}$. Its series index (0=albite…5=anorthite, by 20% steps An0/10/30/50/70/90) is _____.
questions.solution:
**Concept.** Albite(0–10), oligoclase(10–30), andesine(30–50), labradorite(50–70), bytownite(70–90), anorthite(90–100).

**Working.**
$$An_{70}\Rightarrow 4.$$

**Answer.** $4$

questions.subject: Igneous Petrology
questions.stem: A plagioclase has An$_{50}$. Its series index (0=albite…5=anorthite, by 20% steps An0/10/30/50/70/90) is _____.
questions.solution:
**Concept.** Albite(0–10), oligoclase(10–30), andesine(30–50), labradorite(50–70), bytownite(70–90), anorthite(90–100).

**Working.**
$$An_{50}\Rightarrow 3.$$

**Answer.** $3$

questions.subject: Palaeontology
questions.stem: Foraminifera are widely used in petroleum biostratigraphy because they are
options: abundant calcareous microfossils with rapid evolution
questions.solution:
**Concept.** Forams are abundant, environmentally sensitive, fast-evolving microfossils.

**Working.**
Calcareous microfossils.

**Answer.** Calcareous microfossils

questions.stem: Evaluate $\displaystyle\int_0^{5} x^{2}\,dx$.

questions.subject: Sedimentology & Sedimentary Petrology
questions.stem: The progressive loss of porosity in a sandstone with burial is mainly due to
questions.solution:
**Concept.** Mechanical compaction and cementation reduce porosity during diagenesis.

**Working.**
Compaction & cementation.

**Answer.** Compaction and cementation

questions.subject: Structural Geology
questions.stem: A bed strikes $135^\circ$ (right-hand rule) and dips $60^\circ$. The dip direction (azimuth) is _____ degrees.

questions.subject: Engineering & Environmental Geology
questions.stem: A discontinuity ($c=30\,$kPa, $\phi=40^\circ$) carries normal stress $80\,$kPa. Its peak shear strength is _____ kPa.

questions.subject: Engineering & Environmental Geology
questions.stem: A discontinuity ($c=20\,$kPa, $\phi=40^\circ$) carries normal stress $80\,$kPa. Its peak shear strength is _____ kPa.

questions.subject: Structural Geology
questions.stem: A planar fault strikes $150^\circ$ and dips $45^\circ$. The plunge of the true-dip line (steepest line on the plane) is _____ degrees.
questions.solution:
**Concept.** The true-dip line plunges at the dip angle, in the dip direction.

**Working.**
$$\text{plunge}=\text{dip}=45^\circ.$$

**Answer.** $45^\circ$

questions.subject: Structural Geology
questions.stem: A planar fault strikes $30^\circ$ and dips $35^\circ$. The plunge of the true-dip line (steepest line on the plane) is _____ degrees.
questions.solution:
**Concept.** The true-dip line plunges at the dip angle, in the dip direction.

**Working.**
$$\text{plunge}=\text{dip}=35^\circ.$$

**Answer.** $35^\circ$

questions.subject: Structural Geology
questions.stem: At a point $\sigma_x=70$, $\sigma_y=50$, $\tau_{xy}=20\,$MPa. The minor principal stress $\sigma_3$ is _____ MPa.

questions.subject: Structural Geology
questions.stem: A planar fault strikes $120^\circ$ and dips $45^\circ$. The plunge of the true-dip line (steepest line on the plane) is _____ degrees.
questions.solution:
**Concept.** The true-dip line plunges at the dip angle, in the dip direction.

**Working.**
$$\text{plunge}=\text{dip}=45^\circ.$$

**Answer.** $45^\circ$

questions.subject: Structural Geology
questions.stem: A reverse fault forms under a stress regime in which
questions.solution:
**Concept.** Andersonian theory: reverse (thrust) faulting has horizontal $\sigma_1$, vertical $\sigma_3$.

**Working.**
Horizontal $\sigma_1$.

**Answer.** $\sigma_1$ horizontal

questions.subject: Metamorphic Petrology
questions.stem: Which protolith → metamorphic rock pairs are correct?
questions.solution:
**Concept.** Shale→slate, limestone→marble, sandstone→quartzite are correct; granite→basalt is not metamorphic.

**Working.**
Options 1, 2, 3.

**Answer.** Protolith pairs
